official_functions.role: Construct the official Play clientbound set_border_lerp_size fixture
Write, decode, and observe the official border lerp body
Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns set_border_lerp_size to packet id 0x59.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the set_border_lerp_size id to ClientboundSetBorderLerpSizePacket.
The official set_border_lerp_size body is old size double, new size double, then lerp time VarLong.
The fixture uses primitive WorldBorder lerp fields only and does not require initialized Level or runtime world-border state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded lerp fields, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte counts are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove world-border interpolation behavior, world state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer world-border interpolation behavior, world state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
